title: Chemicals and treatment paradigms
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"cn1ma","text":"This protocol is to enhance microglia response and nigral dopaminergic cell death in an acute MPTP model following peripheral inflammation resulting from single i.p. injection of LPS at 2 mg/kg (García-Domínguez et al. 2018).","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Mice were randomly divided into MPTP+LPS+NDP-MSH, MPTP+LPS, and control groups to receive i.p. once daily MPTP . HCl (Millipore Sigma, Cat# M0896; 20 mg/kg) or saline and LPS (Millipore Sigma, Cat# L4391; 1 mg/kg) or PBS from day 1 to day 4.
NDP-MSH (Genscript, Cat# RP10658; 400 µg/kg) or PBS was injected from day 1 to day 12.
Mice were tested for behavioral activities and were sacrificed thereafter on day 12.
Another dose of anti-mouse CD25 monoclonal antibody or isotype control was administered 2 days before the sacrifice.

Animals were treated with anti-mouse CD25 monoclonal antibody (clone PC61, Biolegend, Cat# 102059; 400 µg/mouse) or isotype control (Biolegend, Cat# 401916; 100µg/mice) for 3 alternate days, 1 week before the start of experiment.
Mice were subsequently treated with MPTP, LPS and NDP-MSH as described above.
creator: Fang Zhou
